description_for_human: Access legal documents, run AI analysis playbooks, manage matters, tasks, and communications through Spaarke.
description_for_model: This plugin connects to the Spaarke legal document management platform. Use it when the user asks about legal documents, matters, tasks, deadlines, playbook analysis, financial summaries, or communications. All data is scoped to what the authenticated user is permitted to access.

functions.name: semanticSearch
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to find documents by topic, name, content, or description. Performs hybrid semantic and keyword search across all documents the user has access to. Returns matching documents with relevance scores and snippets.

functions.name: listDocuments
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to see a list of documents, optionally filtered by matter, project, or document type. Use this for browsing documents rather than searching by content.

functions.name: getDocument
functions.description: Call this when the user asks for details about a specific document, such as its metadata, type, matter association, or upload date.

functions.name: getDocumentPreviewUrl
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to view or preview a document. Returns a URL that opens the document in a browser preview.

functions.name: summarizeFile
functions.description: Call this when the user wants a summary of a document's content, key points, or an overview of what a file contains. Returns an AI-generated summary with key points.

functions.name: listChatPlaybooks
functions.description: Call this when the user asks what analysis playbooks or AI capabilities are available for chat. Returns playbooks the user can run in the current context.

functions.name: listPublicPlaybooks
functions.description: Call this when the user asks 'What analysis tools are available?' or wants to browse all publicly shared playbooks. Returns playbooks available to everyone.

functions.name: executePlaybook
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to run a document analysis playbook on a specific document. Starts playbook execution and returns results directly for quick playbooks or a run ID for long-running ones.

functions.name: getPlaybookRunStatus
functions.description: Call this to check the progress of a running playbook execution. Use after executePlaybook returns a run ID for a long-running analysis. Returns current status and progress percentage.

functions.name: getPlaybookRunDetail
functions.description: Call this when a playbook execution has completed and the user wants to see the full results, findings, risk flags, and extracted data.

functions.name: listEvents
functions.description: Call this when the user asks about their tasks, deadlines, due dates, assignments, or upcoming events. Supports filtering by matter, assignee, status, and date range. Use for questions like 'What's due this week?' or 'Show my tasks for matter X'.

functions.name: getEvent
functions.description: Call this when the user asks for details about a specific task, deadline, or event, including its description, logs, and associations.

functions.name: createEvent
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to create a new task, deadline, or appointment. Requires a title and typically a matter association and due date.

functions.name: completeEvent
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to mark a task or event as completed or done.

functions.name: getPortfolio
functions.description: Call this when the user asks for an overview of their matters, projects, or portfolio. Returns summary metrics including total and active matters and projects.

functions.name: getBriefing
functions.description: Call this when the user asks for their daily briefing, morning summary, or wants to know what needs attention today. Returns upcoming deadlines, recent activity, and priority items.

functions.name: getMatterAiSummary
functions.description: Call this when the user asks for a summary of a specific matter, including recent activity, key documents, and current status. Generates an AI-powered matter overview.

functions.name: getMatterFinanceSummary
functions.description: Call this when the user asks about the budget, spending, costs, or invoices for a matter. Returns financial summary including total budget, spend, and invoice count.

functions.name: searchRecords
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to search for Dataverse records such as matters, projects, events, or documents by name or description. Use for entity-level searches rather than document content searches.

functions.name: sendCommunication
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to draft and send an email or other communication, typically related to a matter. Requires recipient, subject, and body.

functions.name: handleAgentMessage
functions.description: Call this to route a conversational message through the Spaarke agent gateway. The gateway determines the appropriate BFF service to handle the request and returns a response with optional Adaptive Card formatting.

functions.name: listAgentPlaybooks
functions.description: Call this to get the list of playbooks available for the agent to offer to users, optionally filtered by document type.

functions.name: runAgentPlaybook
functions.description: Call this to invoke a playbook on a document through the agent gateway. Returns results as an Adaptive Card for quick playbooks or a deep-link for long-running analysis.

functions.name: getAgentPlaybookStatus
functions.description: Call this to check the status of a long-running playbook execution that was started through the agent gateway. Returns progress and result card when complete.

functions.name: createAnalysis
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to start a deep document analysis that requires the Analysis Workspace. Creates an analysis record and optionally begins execution.

functions.name: getAnalysis
functions.description: Call this to check the status and results of a document analysis, including its output, findings, and current state.

functions.name: getCurrentUser
functions.description: Call this when the user asks 'Who am I?' or when you need to identify the current user's profile and permissions.

functions.name: getUserCapabilities
functions.description: Call this to determine what the current user is permitted to do, such as which playbooks they can access and whether they can search documents. Use before offering features the user may not have access to.
